{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | other_designation = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated other_designation }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture other_designation{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi Period|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == Structure and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture other_designations]] [[Category:dd]] The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | other_designation = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated other_designation }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture other_designation{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | other_designation = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated other_designation }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture other_designation{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition[[Category:addition]] of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Manjumori Kofun}}

In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Manjumori Kofun}}

Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Manjumori Kofun}}

File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Yumura.
