The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Manjumori Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:万寿森古墳 up to rev 96778419
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=万寿森古墳 |画像=[[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]湯村3丁目 |位置= {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_scale:10000}} |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=東西31m、南北38m、高さ5m |築造年代=6世紀中葉 |埋葬施設= |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定=山梨県指定史跡「万寿森古墳」 |特記事項= }} '''万寿森古墳'''（まんじゅもりこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]湯村にある[[古墳]]『山梨県の地名』、p.357。形状は[[円墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf 山梨県公報第2582号]}}。 == 立地と地理的・歴史的景観 == [[甲府盆地]]の北縁に位置し、[[湯村山]]南の山裾に立地する。[[標高]]は300メートル付近。 [[荒川 (山梨県)|荒川]]東岸の甲府市湯村・[[千塚 (甲府市)|千塚]]・[[羽黒町 (甲府市)|羽黒町]]・山宮町一帯は古くから[[千塚・山宮古墳群]]と呼ばれる古墳群が濃密に分布する地域として知られる。まず千塚の榎田遺跡が[[弥生時代]]から[[平安時代]]の集落遺跡として知られ、弥生時代の[[方形周溝墓]]が検出されており、古墳後期に最盛期を迎える『山梨県の地名』、p.355。 万寿森古墳の西北西に所在する[[加牟那塚古墳]]は6世紀後半の築造で、[[笛吹市]]御坂町井ノ上の[[姥塚古墳]]に次ぐ山梨県下に第二位の規模の古墳であることで知られる『山梨県の地名』、p.357、p.47。ほか、羽黒町の[[おてんぐさん古墳]]（天狗山古墳）は6世紀代に築造された[[積石塚]]の円墳で、甲府盆地北縁に分布する[[渡来人]]墓制である積石塚の西端に位置づけられている田代・萩原（1984）、p.48。湯村山西麓には大平古墳群が所在している。古代の[[律令制]]下では[[巨摩郡]]に含まれる。 万寿森古墳は湯村山南麓に分布する湯村山古墳群のひとつで、万寿森古墳のほか10数基の古墳が確認されているが『加牟那塚』、p.2、これらの古墳群は多くが開発により消失している。 == 規模と築造年代 == [[File:Manjumori ancient burial mound in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|大正初期頃に撮影された万寿森古墳。南西側より。今日は宅地化が進み、このような角度からの撮影はできない。]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 万寿森古墳の規模は東西31メートル、南北38メートル、高さ5メートル。主体部の保存状態は良好であるが、後述の通り[[江戸時代]]から[[石室]]を利用された痕跡が見られる。主体部は南に開口する両袖形の[[横穴式石室]]で、[[安山岩]]の[[自然石]]による乱石積み（小口積み・横口積み）。石室の部材は50センチから80センチの比較的小型の石材が使われている点が指摘される。 石室の全長は14.2メートル、玄室長7.9メートル、羨道長5.3メートル。主軸は南北。石室幅は奥壁付近で2.44メートル、玄室部の袖側で1.61メートル、羨道入口で1.55メートル。高さは後述の通り[[床|床面]]が後代に[[コンクリート]]舗装されているため正確には不明であるが、最大で3.3メートルを測っており、数10センチの幅が想定されている。 [[副葬品]]や[[埴輪]]・[[葺石]]・[[周溝]]など外部施設は知られておらず、正確な築造年代も不明。近在の甲府市千塚の加牟那塚古墳や、笛吹市御坂町の姥塚古墳では大型石材が用いられているため、万寿森古墳はこれに先行する築造であるとも考えられている。 江戸後期の『[[甲斐国志]]』によれば万寿森古墳は古くから存在を知られており、江戸時代には石室を[[煙硝]]の保管庫として利用し「煙硝蔵」と称されていた。近代にも[[ホテル]]の倉庫として利用されており、開口部には[[扉]]が取り付けられ、床面にはコンクリートが敷かれている。 Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|墳丘・石室開口部（2015年） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 『日本歴史地名大系19 山梨県の地名』[[平凡社]]、[[1995年]] * 田代孝・萩原三男「甲府市域の古墳分布とニ、三の課題」『甲府市史研究 創刊号』1984年 * 石神孝子「万寿森古墳」『山梨県史 資料編1 考古（遺跡）』山梨県、1998年 == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:まんしゆもりこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:甲府市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Manjumori Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]]

The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]] [[Category:甲府市の歴史]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] {{Commons category|Manjumori Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura 3-chome | coordinates = {{Coord|35|40|50.0|N|138|33|8.4|E|region:JP-19_map_size:10000}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天狗山 (甲府市)}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Manjumori Kofun]] [[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Manjumori Kofun}}
