revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.680555555556|N|138.55233333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天狗山 (甲府市)}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] {{Commons category|Manjumori Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q22120416}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Manjumori Kofun]][[ja:万寿森古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Manjumori Kofun | image = [[File:Manjumori Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|WD=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.680555555556|N|138.55233333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天狗山 (甲府市)}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring. File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage, view towards entrance File:Manjumori Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage, view towards main chamber File:Manjumori Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Manjumori Kofun Aug. 2015.JPG|Kofun and stone chamber entrance (2015) == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995}} * Tashiro, Ko & Hagiwara, Mitsuo. "Distribution of Kofun in Kofu City and Related Issues." *Kofu City History Research* Vol. 1, 1984. * Ishigami, Takako. "Manjumori Kofun." *Yamanashi Prefecture History, Archaeology (Sites)*, Yamanashi Prefecture, 1998. == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Manjumori Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|万寿森古墳|version=96778419|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:万寿森古墳]] {{Commons category|Manjumori Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q22120416}} [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|qid=Q201932|1=Kōfu}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.680555555556|N|138.55233333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = East-west: 31m, North-south: 38m, Height: 5m | period = Mid-6th century | burial = | burial_individual = | burial goods = | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Manjumori Kofun''' (万寿森古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Yumura. It has been Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyousei-kk/kouhou/documents/2582.pdf Yamanashi Prefecture Official Gazette No. 2582]}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, on the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Yumura|ja|湯村山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Yumura|qq=}}, at an elevation of approximately 300 meters* {{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. The surrounding area, including Yumura, Chizuka, Haguro, and Yamamiya neighborhoods, is known for the {{ill|Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja|千塚・山宮古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chizuka-Yamamiya Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which features a high concentration of Kofuns. Notable sites include the Enokida ruins in Chizuka, which span from the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, and feature remains such as {{ill|burial Practices of the Yayoi built|lt=rectangular moat tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/burial Practices of the Yayoi built|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487466}} from the Yayoi periodYamanashi Prefecture Place names, p.355. Nearby kofun include the {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamunazuka Kofun|lt=Kamuna Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamuna Kofun|qq=}}, built in the late 6th century and the second largest tumulus in Yamanashi Prefecture, and the {{ill|Mount Tengu|lt=Mount Tengu|lt=Otengu-san Kofun|ja|おてんぐさん古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otengu-san Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天狗山 (甲府市)}}, a 6th-century stone-piled circular mound. These sites are part of the broader landscape associated with immigrant burial traditionsTashiro and Hagiwara (1984), p.48. The Manjumori Kofun is one of several mounds in the Yumura Mountain cluster, many of which have been lost to modern development. == burial and built == [[File:Manjumori ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|250px|Manjumori Kofun, circa early Taisho period.]] [[File:Manjumori Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main Chamber (玄室)}}]] The tumulus measures 31 meters east-west, 38 meters north-south, and 5 meters in height. The burial facility is a south-facing {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} constructed with andesite fieldstones. The chamber features "random stone stacking" (小口積み・横口積み) with smaller stones (50-80 cm). The chamber is 14.2 meters long, with a main chamber (玄室) measuring 7.9 meters and a passage (羨道) of 5.3 meters. Chamber width varies, being 2.44 meters near the rear wall and narrowing to 1.55 meters at the entrance. The chamber height reaches a maximum of 3.3 meters but is partially obscured by later concrete paving. Although no burial goods, haniwa, or surrounding facilities have been found, comparisons with nearby kofun suggest an earlier construction date relative to the large stone-built mounds in the region. The tumulus has been known since the Edo period and was repurposed as a storage facility for gunpowder (煙硝蔵). In modern times, it served as a hotel warehouse, with the addition of doors and concrete flooring.
